asset_type: TaraHome technical blog article cover and 16:9 library card
prompt: Create a clean editorial photograph about diagnosing duplicate Matter smart-home devices: one and only one small unbranded white smart plug is physically installed in a wall outlet while a homeowner seated nearby holds a modern smartphone whose screen shows two simple matching device rows for that same plug. Use a warm lived-in residential living room with natural oak, soft cream plaster, a dark-green cushion or small ceramic accent, and restrained decor. Show the single physical smart plug, the homeowner's hands and phone, and the nearby lamp it controls; the phone screen may show two matching generic device icons but no readable words. Use photorealistic natural editorial photography with realistic materials and proportions, understated premium home-magazine quality, and no glossy advertising treatment. Compose a wide landscape image that remains clear in both a 16:10 article crop and a centered 16:9 card crop; keep the plug and phone near the central safe area in a medium-wide eye-level view. Use soft late-afternoon window light, a calm practical troubleshooting mood, warm beige and natural wood, and restrained deep forest-green accents. Render realistic wood grain, plaster, woven fabric, and ordinary matte plastic. Include exactly one physical smart-home plug. Keep the phone UI inside the real phone screen only. No brand marks, logos, watermark, readable text, embedded headline or title, or invented branded hardware. Avoid fake charts or graphs, decorative routing or network lines, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, infographic styling, duplicated physical devices, glowing sci-fi effects, and generic AI diagram appearance.
